To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding a deeply concerning and unprofessional experience I had at the cashier cage at Sahara Las Vegas.

On March 21,2026 at approximately 22:15. I was in the middle of a transaction with a cage cashier LUCY who had my identification and personal information in her possession. During this time, she began engaging in a separate matter with another customer, dividing her attention while still handling my sensitive information.

I politely asked if she could please complete my transaction first and focus on one customer at a time. In response, she became rude and unprofessional in her tone.

I then requested to file a complaint and spoke with a supervisor identified as MiKE, who was present. Instead of addressing my concern or attempting to resolve the situation, he escalated the matter by involving security. He provided my identification to security personnel and discussed the situation without my consent or any clear communication to me.

Security then proceeded to run my name and information without addressing me directly, explaining the reason, or obtaining my acknowledgment. This raised serious concerns regarding my privacy and how my personal information was handled.

Rather than resolving the issue, I was ultimately asked to leave the property. At no point was I offered any form of resolution, acknowledgment, or customer service recovery.

As a result, I was unable to continue my evening—time during which I would have otherwise spent money at your establishment, as I have done in the past with gaming and dining. Due to this experience, I will not be returning.

I request that this incident be formally reviewed, including the conduct of the cashierLUCY and the supervisor “Mike,” and the actions taken by security. I would also like clarification on your policies regarding customer privacy and the handling of identification.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Jasmine W

inbound_summary: Guest details multiple service inconsistencies during a recent stay including unequal room upgrades between two linked reservations, inequitable express checkout availability, and disparate deposit hold release timing; expresses disappointment and indicates reconsideration of future bookings.
response_summary: No human response found.
reason: Complex, multi-issue service recovery scenario involving room inventory, front desk operations, and billing holds requires cross-departmental investigation and empathetic manual resolution.
inbound_body:
CAUTION: External email, think before you click!

Good afternoon,

My family and I recently stayed at Sahara with two rooms booked under my name. The other room had my brother in law’s name I listed in the event there was a problem and wasn’t at the property. I booked the room that showed on Priceline that showed what looked like the Iconic Alexandria room and I was under the impression that I was booking that for both rooms. Upon arrival, my brother in law’s room had the Iconic upgrades but my room did not. I called down to the front desk about it and was told it was because I didn’t not upgrade to the Iconic Alexandria room. I don’t understand that because if that’s the case neither room should have been upgraded. I let it go because I didn’t want to cause a scene, but that wasn't okay, especially since the trip was funded under my name.

Also, during check out, I had to come down to stand in line to check out. I am on a cane so it’s not the easiest, but it had to be done. The agent was not the friendliest. At all. But really upset even more is that my brother in law was given the option of express check out, which he did not sign up for or opt for. They checked out from their room, no effort and no lines required. Which again makes no sense because the reservation is under my name.

And lastly, funds held deposits have already been released for my brother in law, but ours are still on hold. Does that mean that the release was released for him and not myself being the primary reservation holder?

While the property is beautiful, this did not sit well at all. I hope that you will keep things consistent in the future for your guests. I had a double trip planned and was planning a return trip to Vegas in October, anticipating to stay both times at Sahara because it came so highly recommended by a friend. Honestly, this changed my perspective.
sample_response: Thank you for sharing your feedback about your recent stay. We sincerely apologize for the inconsistencies you experienced with your room assignment, checkout process, and deposit hold. Room categories are subject to availability at check-in, particularly for third-party reservations, and deposit release timeframes vary by financial institution. We have forwarded your concerns to our management team for review, and a guest services representative will follow up with you directly within 24 to 48 hours. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention.
